description.current: Evidence before completion: examine actual evidence (not reports), cite specific proof, match evidence to criteria. Read files, view screenshots, quote output.
issues: UNDER-TRIGGER: Lacks context cue. 'Evidence before completion' is vague — missing WHEN (before what?) or WHO (for whom?) or PROBLEM (what happens if you don't?).
Description uses imperative but feels like a mandate rather than teaching: 'examine actual evidence' → why would someone NOT do this? Needs problem framing first.

issues.description: The Rule (lines 20-29) uses ALL-CAPS rhetoric ('NEVER', 'ALWAYS') 4 times per 10 lines. Effective for emphasis but risks tone of punishment rather than guidance. Works here because preceded by clear 4-step loop, but borders on imperative-spam.
Mandatory Verification Checklist (lines 31-43) has 7 checkboxes, all start with 'DID I' — monotonous rhythm. Stronger if varied: 'DID', 'HAVE I', 'CAN I', 'IS THERE', etc. Minor style issue.

Section 'Rules' (lines 63-71) restates the checklist with 'NEVER/ALWAYS' format. Duplication with earlier rule (lines 20-29) and checklist (lines 31-43). Consider merging or creating hierarchy: Rule → Checklist → Anti-patterns, not Rule → Rule → Checklist.

References point to 'gate-integration-examples.md' which includes CI/CD, parallel agents, recovery — valuable but not all scenarios apply. Skill assumes reader is orchestrating; solo use case less clear.

strengths: The 5-step Verification Loop (lines 48-61) is progressive: IDENTIFY → LOCATE → EXAMINE → MATCH → WRITE. Clear dependencies between steps.
Concrete examples section (lines 45-46) and anti-patterns reference (lines 63-71) ground abstract concept. 'Would a skeptical reviewer agree?' is an excellent gut-check (line 28).
Scope boundary is crisp (lines 16-18): handles completion verification only, defers evidence generation to functional-validation, planning to create-validation-plan.
References (evidence-standards.md, gate-integration-examples.md) contain 10 anti-patterns and real CI/CD examples. High signal-to-noise.
Security policy (lines 76-79) correctly states this skill is read-only, never modifies state.

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: No automation opportunity — this skill is a discipline/mindset skill, not procedural. A script that auto-validates evidence would defeat the point.

top_fixes: Add problem framing to description: 'Ship incomplete work? Mocks pass but users hit crashes? This skill teaches gate discipline: examine every piece of evidence before marking complete. No reports. No delegation. No skip.'
Consolidate rules (lines 20-29, lines 63-71): create one RULE section, reference it, then show CHECKLIST as operationalization.
Rewrite Mandatory Checklist (lines 31-43) with varied sentence starters: 'DID', 'HAVE', 'CAN', 'IS', 'WHAT', 'WOULD'. Same content, better rhythm.
Clarify solo-use case: add 1-2 sentences explaining how this skill applies when you're the validator, not orchestrating agents.

test_prompts.prompt: My iOS team says they've tested the login flow: app builds, logs in, shows dashboard. They're ready to ship. I have 10 minutes to verify before merge. What should I check?
test_prompts.expected_output: Response should invoke gate-validation-discipline. Walk through Verification Loop: IDENTIFY (3 criteria: builds, logs in, shows dashboard) → LOCATE (ask for screenshots, logs, build output) → EXAMINE (read the actual files, view the screenshots) → MATCH (does each file prove one criterion?) → WRITE verdict. Flag if they say 'we ran tests' or 'build succeeded' without you seeing evidence. Mandate: 'Read the evidence, view the screenshots, quote the logs.'

test_prompts.prompt: Sub-agent reports: 'API validation complete, all endpoints respond with 200 OK.' Should I mark it PASS?
test_prompts.expected_output: Response should invoke gate-validation-discipline and flag as incomplete. 'Not without examining the evidence.' Ask: Did you READ the actual response bodies (not just status codes)? Do responses match expected JSON structure? Are there error objects inside 200 responses? Mandate reading evidence-standards.md example (200 OK with error body is a FAIL). Do not mark complete until you've reviewed the actual curl output or response file.

test_prompts.prompt: I've captured 15 screenshots of an iOS feature. I've looked at them all. Can I mark the feature 'complete'?
test_prompts.expected_output: Response should invoke gate-validation-discipline and ask: What specifically did each screenshot prove? (not 'screenshot 5 exists', but 'screenshot 5 shows login form with email field populated after entering text'). Do all criteria have matching evidence? No partial success? No crashes in the log? Emphasize: viewing exists != proof of correctness. Each screenshot must be described in detail and matched to a criterion.
